title: Handle changes in docker compat mode
body:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12830

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: Though, I would like to verify that this doesn't change the Libpod API as well, we have a habit of reusing the compat handlers a lot.
`70-short-names.at` now passes on my laptop. LGTM, assuming that `!= ""` is functionally equivalent to `!= nil` in this use case.
Tests green. @jwhonce please see if this addresses your review comments.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jwhonce*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Sorry, I only noticed this PR after it got merged already: but why do you split on `,`?

If you send this to the official Docker daemon, you get:
`{"message":"cmd=foo,expose=9090 is not a valid change command"}`

If you send `changes=CMD foo,EXPOSE 9090`, it will set CMD to `foo,EXPOSE 9090`.

The correct way is to send `changes=CMD foo\nEXPOSE 9090`, i.e.
`changes=CMD%20foo%0aEXPOSE%209090`
I used the docker command against this and it worked.  But you know this better then me. So I can change it to break on \n.
`docker commit --change 'WORKDIR /' --change 'EXPOSE 9999'` sends `changes=WORKDIR%20&changes=EXPOSE%209090`, what did you run?
Note that https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.41/#operation/ImageCommit says "instructions to apply while committing", so having them newline-separated seems natural.
